text:
Once, there was a cat who could change colors. This cat lived in a quaint little house on the edge of the forest, full of all sorts of curious creatures. One afternoon, while sunbathing under an ancient oak tree, the feline pondered its extraordinary ability.

It decided that it would go trick-or-treating. Instead of ordinary colors, the cat collected berries of every hue—violet, neon green, and deep indigo. As it traipsed around the village, houses offered not candy, but berry samples. The cat nibbled on each, experiencing tastes so unique that every home seemed a new adventure.

Back at home, the cat sat by a window watching the world go by. Suddenly, it spotted a strange creature, a creature that shifted colors as it moved. It was a changeling, a mythical being adept at such illusions. The cat approached slowly, curious.

The changeling, intrigued by the curious feline, started to dance. Each move changed
